skill.name: receiving-code-review
skill.description: Guides agents through code review feedback by verifying suggestions, clarifying unclear requests, and responding with technical rigor before making changes.
skill.summary: Evaluate review feedback carefully before implementing code changes.
skill.author: ZhanlinCui

security_audit.summary: The static backtick, weak cryptography, and network reconnaissance findings are false positives from Markdown fences or plain prose. The only confirmed operational risk is a low-risk instruction to use GitHub CLI for inline review replies, which may execute an external command and make a GitHub API request in a user-authorized workflow.

low_findings.title: GitHub CLI Reply Instruction Uses External Command
low_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to reply to inline review comments with `gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{pr}/comments/{id}/replies`. This is a legitimate GitHub workflow command, but it can perform a network request and should run only with user-authorized repository context.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line explicitly names a GitHub CLI API command, so external command and network risk factors are real. Confidence is below very high because the command is scoped to normal review replies and does not show credential theft or unauthorized endpoints.

low_findings.title: Static Backtick Execution Findings Are Markdown Fences
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution locations are Markdown code fences and prose examples. No executable Ruby, shell script, or dynamic command substitution appears at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context shows fenced Markdown examples and workflow pseudocode, not executable backtick substitution. The skill contains documentation only and no script file.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are Substring False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography locations are ordinary prose in the skill description and a review response example. No cryptographic algorithm, hash function, encryption API, or security-sensitive implementation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both locations are natural-language instructions or sample wording. There is no code path that invokes MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or another weak cryptographic primitive.

low_findings.title: Network Reconnaissance Finding Is Prose
low_findings.description: The reported network reconnaissance location says to use technical reasoning when pushing back on feedback. It does not instruct port scanning, host discovery, service probing, or network enumeration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a bullet about communication style, not a network operation. No reconnaissance tool, target, address, or scanning workflow appears in the cited text.

dangerous_patterns.title: External GitHub API Command Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes a `gh api` command pattern for replying to GitHub pull request review comments. This is not malicious, but it can modify remote GitHub state when the agent has valid credentials.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command pattern is explicit and would use GitHub authentication if executed. The risk is limited because it targets review-comment replies rather than arbitrary data transfer.

content.value_statement: Code review feedback can be unclear, incomplete, or technically wrong for the current codebase. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to verify feedback, ask precise questions, and implement changes carefully.
actual_capabilities: Provides a step-by-step pattern for reading, understanding, verifying, evaluating, and responding to review feedback.
Tells the agent to ask for clarification before implementing unclear multi-item feedback.
Defines checks for external reviewer suggestions, including context, compatibility, and existing behavior.
Prioritizes review fixes by blocking issues, simple fixes, and complex changes.
Gives response examples for correct feedback, pushback, YAGNI decisions, and unclear requests.
Specifies GitHub inline thread replies through the GitHub CLI API path.
limitations: Does not inspect a repository by itself; the agent must still read and test the actual code.
Does not provide automated review parsing, CI integration, or patch generation scripts.
Cannot decide product or architecture tradeoffs without user context.
GitHub reply guidance requires an authenticated GitHub CLI and appropriate repository permissions.

use_cases.title: Clarify Ambiguous Review Requests
use_cases.description: Use the skill when a reviewer gives multiple requested changes and some items are unclear or dependent on each other.
use_cases.target_user: Solo developer using Claude or Codex

use_cases.title: Evaluate External Contributor Feedback
use_cases.description: Use the skill to check whether external suggestions fit the codebase before accepting or rejecting them.

use_cases.title: Keep Agent Responses Technical
use_cases.description: Use the skill to make AI coding agents respond with concise technical reasoning instead of performative agreement.
use_cases.target_user: Engineering team lead configuring Claude Code workflows

prompt_templates.title: Review One Comment
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner triage for a single review comment
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the receiving-code-review process to this review comment. Restate the requested change, verify it against the code, and tell me whether to implement, clarify, or push back.

prompt_templates.title: Clarify Multiple Items
prompt_templates.scenario: Handling a review with several requested fixes
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the receiving-code-review workflow on these review items. Identify which items are clear, which need clarification, and which should be implemented first after verification.

prompt_templates.title: Assess External Feedback
prompt_templates.scenario: Checking feedback from an external reviewer
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this external review feedback for this repository. Check whether each suggestion is technically correct, whether it breaks existing behavior, and whether YAGNI applies.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare GitHub Replies
prompt_templates.prompt: After verifying the requested changes, draft concise GitHub inline replies for each review comment. Use technical acknowledgments, note any justified pushback, and avoid top-level summary replies unless needed.

output_examples.input: Reviewer says to remove legacy compatibility code.
output_examples.output: The agent checks supported platform versions first, then explains whether the legacy path is still required or should be removed.

output_examples.input: Reviewer asks for metrics tracking, filters, and export support on an unused endpoint.
output_examples.output: The agent searches for endpoint usage and asks whether to remove the endpoint instead of building unused features.

output_examples.input: User says to fix six review items, but two are unclear.
output_examples.output: The agent identifies the clear items, asks about the unclear ones, and waits before making partial changes.

best_practices: Verify each review suggestion against the current codebase before editing files.
Clarify unclear review items before implementing any related changes.
Test each fix individually and check for regressions before moving to the next item.
anti_patterns: Accepting review feedback because it sounds authoritative without checking the code.
Implementing only the clear parts of a multi-item review while leaving unclear dependencies unresolved.
Adding professional-looking features when the codebase has no real usage for them.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps AI coding agents handle code review feedback with verification, clarification, and technical judgment.

faq.question: Does it automatically apply code changes?
faq.answer: No. It provides a decision process. The agent still needs to inspect files, edit code, and run tests.

faq.question: Can it push back on reviewer comments?
faq.answer: Yes. It guides the agent to push back when feedback is technically incorrect, unsafe, or not needed.

faq.question: Is it safe for external pull request reviews?
faq.answer: Yes, with normal caution. External suggestions should be verified against the repository before implementation.

faq.question: Does it require GitHub access?
faq.answer: Only the GitHub thread reply guidance requires GitHub CLI access. The core review workflow does not require it.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
